description: filename fast path: when the query literally names a PDF in the folder, extract+rank its pages by query-token frequency and emit a top-10 ranking + top page text (read-only over the PDFs)
rules: read-only: extracts page text from the named PDFs via the retriever's bundled pdfium stage, never mutates the PDFs or any index
only fires when the query string verbatim contains a PDF basename (stem >= 6 chars, with or without .pdf, case-insensitive); otherwise emits NO_MATCH
ranking is deterministic: pages scored by summed query-token occurrence count, top 10 returned 1-indexed, plus the top page's first 4000 chars
usage: Set QUERY (the user's question, which must literally contain a PDF basename for the fast path to fire) and PDFS_DIR (the folder of source PDFs). Output: filename_fast_path.txt — either NO_MATCH (no basename in query), NO_TEXT (matched but image-only PDFs), or a JSON {ranking:[{doc_id,page_number,rank}]} line followed by ---TOP_PAGE_TEXT--- and the top page's raw text.
limitation: When a basename matches it invokes the NeMo Retriever `retriever pdf stage page-elements --method pdfium` extractor, which requires the NeMo Retriever venv on PATH; without it the extraction step degrades to empty output. The NO_MATCH path (no basename in the query) runs fully offline. Does not embed, vector-search, or re-rank — that is the external `retriever query` CLI.
